name: National Security Innovation Society at Berkeley
summary: The National Security Innovation Society at Berkeley Society bridges technology and policy to address emerging security challenges. We connect students with experts through research, events, and projects that prepare the next generation of leaders.

name: Association of Latino Professionals for America at Berkeley (ALPFA)
summary: The mission of the Association of Latino Professionals for America (ALPFA) is to empower the Latine community at UC Berkeley by offering extensive leadership training, essential career advancement resources, and valuable networking opportunities.

name: UnifIBD at Berkeley
summary: Our mission is to raise awareness, offer support, and push research forward so others can better understand and manage IBD, with an end goal of improving the lives of everyone affected by these complex diseases.

name: DataStory
summary: As data science emerges as one of the most in-demand professions today, DataStory provides an environment for students to get a taste of what a future in the industry would look like.

name: Legal Innovation and Entrepreneurship at Berkeley
summary: LINE fosters a dynamic and inclusive environment, bridging the gap between law & tech. We plan to promote awareness about legal innovation and tech solutions, enhance legal education, to promote entrepreneurship, and drive professional development.

name: Healthcare Business Association at Berkeley
summary: We serve as a professional space for students of the healthcare business community to spark ideas, encourage questions, and build a lifelong network.

name: HOSA Future Health Professionals of Berkeley 💙
summary: HOSA FHP of Berkeley serves to inspire and support pre-health students at UCB. As a National HOSA chapter, this club involves volunteering, educational symposiums and conferences, competitive & recognition events, and leadership opportunities.

name: Middle Eastern Business Association
summary: MEBA strives to be an organization that provides Middle Eastern students with opportunities to grow and establish connections in the business sector.

name: Google Developer Student Club at Berkeley
summary: Our goal is for students who are interested in Google developer technologies to grow their knowledge in a peer-to-peer learning environment and build solutions for local businesses and their community.

name: Campus Dept | Berkeley Arts + Design
summary: The UC Berkeley Arts + Design Initiative features, fortifies, and mobilizes existing excellence in the arts and design at Berkeley.

name: Paper Crane
summary: Paper Crane aims to create a support and relief system for hospitalized children by providing expressive art workshops at hospitals.

name: Law Students for Climate Accountability at Berkeley
summary: LSCA Berkeley’s purpose is to activate and mobilize the power of Berkeley Law students to transform the legal industry’s role from exacerbating climate injustice to meaningfully supporting a just transition.

name: Scholars of Finance
summary: Scholars of Finance is a nonprofit organization dedicated to developing undergrad finance students into the next generation of highly capable financial executives - who will act and lead with integrity and a commitment to serving the greater good.

name: BrainTrust
summary: Through the facilitation of peer support group meetings, a one-on-one buddy volunteer program, and lectures by physicians and researchers, BrainTrust seeks to create a powerful social support network for individuals with brain injury.

name: Paragon Consulting at Berkeley
summary: Paragon Consulting provides BIPOC-owned small businesses in the Bay Area innovative and creative solutions to industry-defining challenges, while providing opportunities for a diverse group of undergraduate students.

name: PrioriT
summary: Our purpose is to create a vibrant, inclusive space where all students—especially continuing and international admits—can thrive socially, professionally, and personally.

name: GENup at Berkeley (formerly known as GENup Collegiate at Berkeley)
summary: GENup Collegiate at Berkeley advocates for educational issues through the power of youth voices in key policy efforts on the California education system and unites diverse student activists in a “Generation Upward” movement.

name: International Student Mentorship Program at Berkeley 💙
summary: The purpose of this organization is to mentor international students academically, professionally, and personally, including adjusting to culture and life in the US and equipping them for career growth in the future.

name: Design at Berkeley 💙
summary: Our mission is to make design more accessible. Design at Berkeley has fostered a growing and welcoming community of anyone interested in design through workshops, events, and resources.

name: SEAONC at Berkeley
summary: SEAONC at Berkeley will be an organization committed to connecting graduate students in the Structural Engineering, Mechanics, & Materials program with relevant, professional, academic resources.

name: Myopia Control Club at Berkeley (MCC) 💙
summary: Myopia Control Club aims to introduce students to the clinical aspects of evidence-based myopia control. We hold seminars and workshops with hands-on learning regarding the various modalities of myopia control such as atropine and corrective lenses.

name: Brain Exercise Initiative at Berkeley 💙
summary: We are a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that uses simple math, writing and reading aloud exercises as an intervention to improve cognitive function in those with Alzheimer's Disease while encouraging intergenerational collaboration.

name: Ideas on Consciousness 💙
summary: Ideas on Consciousness is a student organization focused on providing spaces for students of different disciplines and backgrounds to cross paths, share ideas, and explore questions relating to the nature of consciousness.

name: Global Research and Consulting Group at Berkeley
summary: Global Research and Consulting Group: UC Berkeley chapter of the global nonprofit consultancy advancing social impact in healthcare, education, sustainability, and equity through strategic research and data-driven recommendations for NGOs and enterprises

name: Latine Social Work Caucus (Formerly known as Latinx Social Work Caucus)
summary: The purpose of the Latine Social Work Caucus (LSWC) is to build connections that ensure students and faculty in the School of Social Welfare feel a sense of belonging and community.

name: Cal Boxing
summary: Cal Boxing welcomes student athletes with all levels of experience to reach their full potential as boxers. We compete in intercollegiate boxing events sanctioned by USA Boxing.

name: Cal Women’s Ultimate (Pie Queens)
summary: Also known as the Pie Queens, we are a program that strives to introduce people to ultimate frisbee and create a great community. We travel a fair amount to tournaments throughout the year and have multiple teams to fit anyone’s schedule!

name: Transfers at Berkeley Law 💙
summary: TABL is committed to addressing the needs of both 2L and 3L transfer students through career and academic advising, mentorship, social activities, networking, and other events and programs.

name: Financial Engineering Students Association
summary: We aim to expose its members to the finance and data science industry in order to network and establish relationships that will lead to jobs in the field.

name: Pathways to Community Service
summary: The purpose of our organization is to help students navigate volunteer opportunities on campus and surrounding communities through an up-to-date database. We hope to make it easier for students to discover, and thus get involved in community service.

name: Atrium
summary: Atrium is a magazine dedicated to creative student exploration of the intersection between the sciences and human experience.

name: Paleontological Society of Berkeley 💙
summary: The Paleontological Society of Berkeley aims to connect students interested in paleontology with each other and the UC paleontological community at large.

name: Civil and Environmental Engineering For Anti-Racism 💙
summary: We aim to build on historical efforts in the student community and to push for immediate and sustained action to address structural racism in the Civil and Environmental Engineering department.

name: Undergraduate Business Group
summary: Undergraduate Business Group aims to foster a close-knit community of business students on campus through career mentorship, professional training, and social activities.

name: Bike Builders of Berkeley
summary: To build a bike from the ground up as a team. Throughout this process we will explore bike design, fabrication, and assembly.

name: DX Partners
summary: Our mission is to educate students on campus about investment strategies (from sourcing valuable stocks to pitching) and to generate stable returns for our fund based on fundamental valuation in the equity market.

name: Association of Women in EECS 💙
summary: AWE is a student-run organization at UC Berkeley that seeks to empower female and non-binary undergraduate students pursuing Computer Science and/or Electrical Engineering disciplines.

name: LSBE Student Board
summary: The purpose of our organization is to provide student representation for members both in an interested in the Robinson Life Sciences, Business and Entrepreneurship program.

name: NODE 💙
summary: NODE’s goal is to increase incoming numbers and retention of URM graduate students at UC Berkeley in the Chemistry Department by providing mentorship and building a better community to aid in supporting our graduate students.

name: LAHBA at Haas 💙
summary: LAHBA at Haas aka Latin American and Hispanic Business Association

name: African Entrepreneurs at Berkeley
summary: Empowering Berkeley's next generation of builders to shape Africa's future.

name: SubFrame (formerly known as Altruistic Artificial Intelligence (AI) at Berkeley)
summary: SubFrame explores how humans and AI produce meaning behind the frames!

name: Korean Juris Doctors (Formerly Global Student Association at Berkeley Law) 💙
summary: The purpose of the organization is twofold: first, to make it easier for current and future international students to navigate various career paths; and second, to build a vibrant network of legal professionals, practitioners, and academics.

name: Firework Band
summary: We are a Chinese contemporary band based in Berkeley. https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV1yo4y137bC/?share_source=copy_web

name: Project Sunshine at Berkeley 💙
summary: Volunteer to bring joy and play to meet the psychosocial and developmental needs of pediatric patients!

name: AI Safety at Berkeley
summary: We're a community of students interested in building robust, transparent, and controllable AI systems.

name: CASCADE: Community Service
summary: CASCADE is a mentorship organization dedicated to developing a culture of compassion, service and empathy.

name: Standup Comedy (at Berkeley) 💙
summary: To provide UC Berkeley students with an accessible outlet to explore stand-up comedy through collaboration, mentorship, and performance opportunities.

name: Biotech @Berkeley 💙
summary: Biotech @Berkeley seeks to connect and represent all graduate students from UC Berkeley interested in biotechnology. Our group aims to create a student community and gateway to exploring biotech-focused resources and opportunities at Berkeley.

name: Chinese Undergraduate Pre-Law Student Association
summary: The Chinese Undergraduate Pre-Law Student Association (CUPLSA) serves to build a community among Chinese international students who are currently on the Pre-Law track or are interested in law and applying to a law school.

name: The Dialectic 💙
summary: The Dialectic promotes an open space where members of the UC Berkeley community can advance their political knowledge and opinions through dialogue, panels, debates and other events, whilst sharing this knowledge with the greater public through media.

name: LEADers - Leaders for Equity & Democracy in Education
summary: We work to engage passionate, equity-conscious, practicing leaders with the theories, research design principles, and networking opportunities.

name: UX @ Berkeley
summary: Teach the disciplines of UI/UX, which include design thinking, prototyping, and user testing. Work on client-led projects to create and prepare a portfolio for internships using professional tools with advice from industry experts.

name: Aspiring Educators and Student Affairs Professionals at Berkeley (Formerly known as Aspiring Educators at Berkeley) 💙
summary: AESAP (Aspiring Educators and Student Affairs Professionals) Berkeley is UC Berkeley's first and only pre-professional education organization. It is dedicated to students interested in careers in K-12 or higher education!

name: Diaspora Magazine
summary: Diaspora Magazine serves to provide a safe space for Black students to express creativity through journalism and art. We aim to amplify Black voices and share those perspectives with the broader campus community.

name: Behavioral Economics Association at Berkeley
summary: Serve as a common meeting ground for extraordinary minds interested in Behavioral Economics, research, or experiment design.

name: The IT Club at Berkeley 💙
summary: The IT Club strives to address the global e-waste problem by promoting right-to-repair, offering computer repair/upgrade services, and refurbishing disused computers for underserved students. We also provide free IT services to Berkeley students!

name: SEMM DSR
summary: Build community among doctoral students and researchers (DSRs) in the Structural Engineering, Mechanics, and Materials (SEMM) program of the Civil and Environmental Engineering (CEE) department at UC Berkeley.

name: NewSpace at Berkeley
summary: NewSpace@Berkeley connects the Berkeley student community to the emerging commercial space industry. We aim to connect and educate students from all majors about the opportunities currently available to them in the NewSpace economy

name: Ukrainian Club of Berkeley 💙
summary: Our organization seeks to unite members of the UC Berkeley community who share a Ukrainian identity or an interest in Ukrainian history, culture or who simply stand with Ukraine

name: Native American & Indigenous Business Association 💙
summary: NA&IBA is a community of business students that seeks to promote the success of Native American & Indigenous students, create awareness around Native issues, and foster a positive culture of unity, cooperation, and respect.

name: Core Consulting Group 💙
summary: The Core Consulting Group aims to grant equal professional development opportunities to UC Berkeley students, including transfers, that want to pursue a career in consulting.

name: Haas Evening and Weekend MBA Association
summary: The Haas EWMBA has been created by the students of the Evening and Weekend MBA program at Berkeley’s Haas School of Business to be the official representative of the EWMBA student body and its agent to optimize and enrich the EWMBA program experience.

name: Sustainable Entrepreneurship at Berkeley
summary: Sustainable Entrepreneurship At Berkeley is a UC Berkeley-based professional development network initiative that hopes to connect, educate, and mentor students interested in impact investing and the sustainable startup sector.

name: Mass Media at Berkeley Law
summary: Mass Media at Berkeley Law (MMaBL) is a group for law students interested in the intersection of media law and journalism.

name: Real Talk Buddies
summary: Real Talk Buddies supports undergraduate students by providing them the opportunity to receive mentorship from graduate student mentors.

name: Nuclear Is Clean Energy Club 💙
summary: We aim to engage with club members, UC Berkeley students, and members of the public about nuclear reactors, waste, and technology.

name: Keebs at Berkeley 💙
summary: Keebs at Berkeley aims to be an organization for people to socialize and learn more about computer-aided design, basic electrical engineering, and appreciation of aesthetics through a view of a hobby: mechanical keyboards.

name: Frontera at Berkeley
summary: With Immigration and border policy becoming Key agenda Items United States politics; Frontera at Berkeley looks for solutions to the hardest policy problems, raises awareness on the "fronterice" Identity, and the southern border migrant experience.

name: Diversity and Inclusion in Real Estate
summary: DAIRE's mission is to provide a unique hands-on experience for Underrepresented Minorities to learn about real estate through both our education series and junior member education series in which it teaches students how to evaluate REITs.

name: Student Association for Environmental Justice and Health Equity 💙
summary: SA-EJHE aims to empower students to connect environmental justice and health equity by investigating the science behind environmental determinants of health and advocating in fair and just practice.

name: Haas Blockchain Club 💙
summary: The Haas Blockchain Club is committed to providing members with educational and professional opportunities in the blockchain industry.

name: DAES’ Black Film Collective
summary: We strive to provide a space for Black students to create films, watch films made by Black creators, and to learn about the process of filmmaking. We also aim to give Black students the chance to build their portfolios for future careers in entertainment.

name: Career Readiness Program at Berkeley 💙
summary: CRP at Berkeley aims to empower underprivileged students with career experience, by creating a platform that bridges the gap between education and the work industry, paving the way for a new wave of industry leaders.

name: International Software Product Management Association at Berkeley
summary: Software Product at Cal is the first North American chapter of the International Software Product Management Association. SPC intends to educate and provide experiences in product management as well as create a community of like-minded individuals.

name: Product Management Club of Berkeley Haas 💙
summary: Help students become product leaders who drive change and embody the Haas leadership principles.

name: Women on Wall Street at Berkeley 💙
summary: Women on Wall Street at Berkeley (WOW) is Berkeley's first and only independent student organization for women and gender nonbinary students interested in finance

name: Entrepreneur Through Acquisition Club 💙
summary: The ETA Club is dedicated to educate the Haas community on entrepreneurship through acquisition and empower future business owners with a network of searchers, investors and current operators.

name: The Beauty Hub at Berkeley 💙
summary: We aim to provide students with a “hub” of local beauty products and services, strengthen beauty businesses through entrepreneurial mentorship, and deliver beauty-related content catered to students!

name: BIA at Berkeley
summary: BIA at Berkeley is on a mission to co-create benefits with all students at Berkeley. We provide free services and campus information to incoming students. Our free, open membership welcomes everyone to enjoy discounts & to attend recreational activities.

name: Surge - Electric Motorcycles at Berkeley
summary: Student Team Building Electric Motorcycles For Competition

name: Piedmont Consulting Group
summary: Our primary objective is to provide quality consulting services to our clients while fostering an inclusive community. Our aim with each client collaboration is to deliver a substantial impact.

name: Notion at Berkeley
summary: Notion’s Campus Leaders Program seeks to empower students across the world to connect, share, and build community through Notion.

name: Central Valley at Berkeley
summary: Central Valley at Berkeley's mission is to champion education equity in the Central Valley through mentoring, tutoring, and resource provision, creating pathways for low income, POC, and undocumented students to access higher education.

name: Female Founders and Funders of Berkeley
summary: Our mission is to empower women founders and funders at Berkeley by fostering a supportive community and providing essential resources for connection, learning, and growth to drive innovation and equality in the startup and VC industries.

name: Biophysics Club
summary: Biophysics club will facilitate networking and community building within the biophysics and quantitative biology graduate programs at UC Berkeley by hosting networking, informational, and social events.

name: Expansion Pictures
summary: Expansion Pictures is a student organization that supports passion for filmmaking, animation, & cinematic exploration. EP provides an inclusive space for experimentation, discussion & showcase of media. We nurture collaboration & expansion of ideas.

name: Atlas Strategy Group
summary: Atlas Strategy Group is a non-profit consulting firm consisting of analysts from the Robinson Life Sciences, Business, and Entrepreneurship Program (LSBE) dedicated to tackling the unique problems our clients face with novel, strategy-backed solutions.

name: Undergraduate Law Review at Berkeley
summary: Our primary purpose is to create a supportive environment where undergraduates can explore the ever-evolving field of law.

name: Perennial QT Film Club 💙
summary: Our purpose is to write and produce short films with a focus on LGBTQ+ representation and themes. We intend to discuss issues of queer and trans representation in media and use that to create our own original short film scripts.

name: UndocuStudents at Berkeley Law
summary: UndocuStudents at Berkeley Law seeks to empower undocumented law students pursuing their legal education. UndocuStudents offers resources to assist students with professional opportunities, academics, financial aid, and legal services.

name: Representation of Asian and Pacific Islander in the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering (RAPID-CEE) 💙
summary: We aim to foster academic growth, professional development, and cultural exchange, while providing a platform for collaboration, mentorship, and support for API graduate students in CEE.

name: MERGE Labels 💙
summary: Music Exploration Receptive to Genres and Engagement - A mock music label at UC Berkeley, giving prospective music artists, artist managers, producers, songwriters, designers, filmmakers and more the opportunity to learn, experience, and grow.

name: ColorStack at Berkeley 💙
summary: We exist to increase the number of Black and Latinx Computer Science graduates that go on to launch rewarding technical careers.

name: Astrophotography at Berkeley 💙
summary: AaB fosters a judgment-free environment for students of all familiarities interested in astronomy and telescopic observation! Club activities include trips to areas of low light pollution, group socials, and astrophotography tutorials. Come explore!

name: Political Economy Student Association 💙
summary: With the support of the Political Economy department, we aim to serve the Political Economy student community by connecting them to other students, the department, and the career opportunities offered by the Political Economy Alumni Advisory Board.

name: Critical Pacific Island Studies Collective (CPISC)
summary: The Critical Pacific Islands Studies Collective (CPISC) brings together a diverse group of researchers at UC Berkeley to foster a growing academic network of Pacific Islanders and allies.
